commands.review_summary:
The PR marks resolved MCP transports as OAuth-backed, recreates stale streamable HTTP MCP sessions after OAuth 401 failures, and adds regression coverage for catalog refresh, tool-call retry, and backoff preservation.

PR surface: Source +68, Tests +344. Total +412 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for source-level reproduction: the linked report gives concrete token-expiry steps, current main reuses persistent MCP sessions, and the pinned SDK can throw StreamableHTTPError(401) after successful auth on the old streamable HTTP session. I did not run a live one-hour current-main expiry test in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The remaining action is auth-provider/runtime maintainer acceptance and candidate selection, not a narrow automated repair.

target.title: fix: recover MCP OAuth sessions after token refresh
target.body:
Closes #98377.

## What Problem This Solves
Long-running gateway MCP sessions could stay stuck after OAuth access-token expiry when the refreshed bearer was rejected on the old streamable HTTP MCP session.

## Why This Change Was Made
The session MCP runtime now treats OAuth streamable HTTP 401s as stale-session failures, retires the current MCP transport once, rebuilds the catalog/session, and replays the request through the existing refresh-token path. Static-header and non-OAuth MCP failures keep the existing failure/backoff behavior.

## User Impact
Headless gateways can recover OAuth-protected remote MCP servers without requiring a manual `openclaw mcp login` while a valid refresh token exists.

## Evidence
Added deterministic streamable HTTP OAuth regressions for catalog refresh, direct tool calls, and failed retry/backoff preservation.

Real gateway proof with `startGatewayServer`, the QA mock provider, and a local streamable-http OAuth MCP server. The server expired the access token immediately after gateway cataloging, then rejected the refreshed bearer on the stale MCP session:

```text
gateway proof result: OpenClaw gateway response completed after OAuth expiry
gateway proof final: MCP_CODE_MODE_FILE_OK note=fixture-note-alpha unclear=none improvement=virtual-api-files-were-clear-and-needed-one-exec
gateway proof counts: token_refreshes=1 initialize_calls=2
redacted OAuth/MCP server transcript:
- initialize session=- token=initial-access -> 200 stale-session
- notifications/initialized session=stale-session token=initial-access -> 202
- tools/list session=stale-session token=initial-access -> 200
- server/expire session=stale-session token=initial-access -> access token expired after catalog
- tools/call session=stale-session token=initial-access -> 401 invalid_token
- POST /token session=- token=refresh-token -> 200 refreshed-access
- tools/call session=stale-session token=refreshed-access -> 401 stale_mcp_session
- initialize session=- token=refreshed-access -> 200 fresh-session
- notifications/initialized session=fresh-session token=refreshed-access -> 202
- tools/list session=fresh-session token=refreshed-access -> 200
- tools/call session=fresh-session token=refreshed-access -> 200 fixture-note-alpha:fresh-session
```
target.author: turbotheturtle
